title: QFINHUB Reply Accelerator Prospects — Batch 2
strategy: Smaller blogs, resource pages, real estate/housing blogs, credit unions with education pages. Prioritize reply likelihood over domain authority.

prospects_from_held_batch1.name: Can I Retire Yet
prospects_from_held_batch1.note: Good fit for retirement content if pitched differently. Retirement-focused audience = natural retirement calculator embed fit.

prospects_from_held_batch1.name: Go Curry Cracker
prospects_from_held_batch1.note: International FIRE audience. Global housing angle relevant.

prospects_from_held_batch1.name: Budget Simple Life
prospects_from_held_batch1.note: Debt-free minimalist finance — widget fits their budget-conscious audience.

new_prospects.name: Afford Anything
new_prospects.reply_likelihood: HIGH — Paula Pant's blog, active community. Small team, owner-operated feel.
new_prospects.widget_fit: Afford Anything's thesis is 'you can afford anything but not everything' — mortgage affordability widget is PERFECTLY aligned with her content philosophy.
new_prospects.pitch_angle: Philosophical alignment: 'Your core message is about making intentional choices — our widget helps readers make the biggest financial choice of their lives with clarity.'

new_prospects.name: The College Investor
new_prospects.reply_likelihood: HIGH — Robert Farrington's blog, active community. Frequently features financial tools.
new_prospects.widget_fit: Millennial/Gen Z audience making first home purchases. Budget planner + mortgage affordability combo pitch.
new_prospects.pitch_angle: First-time homebuyer angle: 'Your audience is entering peak homebuying years — our free widget gives them a real number, not a guess.'

new_prospects.name: ESI Money
new_prospects.reply_likelihood: HIGH — Solo blogger, millionaire interviews, actively responds to emails.
new_prospects.widget_fit: High-net-worth audience making strategic housing decisions. Compound interest + retirement calculators combo pitch.
new_prospects.pitch_angle: Wealth building angle: 'Your readers are building wealth intentionally — our free calculators help them optimize their biggest expense: housing.'

new_prospects.name: Millennial Revolution
new_prospects.reply_likelihood: HIGH — Kristy and Bryce, FIRE bloggers, active community. Personal brand.
new_prospects.widget_fit: FIRE audience making housing decisions that affect FIRE timeline. Mortgage affordability directly relevant.
new_prospects.pitch_angle: FIRE math angle: 'Housing is the #1 variable in FIRE math — our widget shows readers exactly what home price keeps them on track.'

new_prospects.name: PenFed Credit Union — Financial Education
new_prospects.reply_likelihood: MEDIUM — Credit union education center. Formal but may feature tools.
new_prospects.widget_fit: Credit union members making mortgage decisions. Affordability + mortgage calculator combo.
new_prospects.pitch_angle: Member education angle: 'Your education center helps members make informed decisions — our free widget is a natural fit for your mortgage resources page.'

send_priority: Afford Anything (new, highest reply likelihood + perfect widget alignment)
The College Investor (new, features tools frequently)
Can I Retire Yet (held from B1, retirement audience fit)
held: Go Curry Cracker — hold for batch 3
Budget Simple Life — hold for batch 3
PenFed — hold for batch 3 (credit union needs more formal pitch)
do_not_send_until: Phase 16.10 deploy complete + Auditor >=90 on all
